title: Correcting Inconsistent Type Inference for a Generic Math Calculator

statement.markdown: Implement a generic math calculator that performs addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division. The calculator should accept two numbers and an operator as input. The calculator should throw an error if the input operator is not valid. The calculator should handle division by zero correctly. However, the type inference for the calculator is inconsistent, resulting in type errors when using the calculator with different data types. Correct the type inference and fix the calculator to work correctly with different data types.
statement.notes: The calculator should handle division by zero correctly, and the type inference should be consistent across all data types.

hints.text: Check the type inference for the calculator and ensure it is consistent across all data types.
Use the correct data types for the input numbers and operator.
Handle division by zero correctly and throw an error if necessary.

editorial.title: Correcting Inconsistent Type Inference for a Generic Math Calculator
editorial.intuition: The calculator should use a generic type to handle different data types. The calculator should use a switch statement to handle the different operators.
editorial.bruteForce: The calculator can use a brute-force approach to handle the different operators, but this will result in inconsistent type inference.
editorial.betterSolution: The calculator can use a generic type and a switch statement to handle the different operators, resulting in consistent type inference.
editorial.optimalSolution: The calculator should use a generic type and a switch statement to handle the different operators, and it should use a try-catch block to handle division by zero.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The calculator should pass all test cases and handle division by zero correctly.
editorial.dryRun: The calculator should pass the dry run test case and handle division by zero correctly.
commonMistakes: Division by zero not handled correctly

functionSignature.javascript: function calculator(a: number, b: number, operator: string): number | string;
functionSignature.python: def calculator(self, a: int, b: int, operator: str) -> int | str:
